previous_statement: ARITHMETIC ABSORPTION eta <= sqrt(eta), hence a single eps=C sqrt(eta). Claim: for eta in [0,eta_0] with eta_0<=1, eta <= sqrt(eta). Proof: if eta=0 both sides are 0. If 0<eta<=1 then eta^2 <= eta (multiply 0<eta<=1 by eta>0: eta*eta <= eta*1), and taking nonnegative square roots (sqrt monotone on [0,infty)) gives eta = sqrt(eta^2) <= sqrt(eta). Consequence: the O(eta) defects from nodes 1.3 (C_1 eta), 1.4 (C_2 eta), 1.5 (C_3 eta) satisfy C_i eta <= C_i sqrt(eta) (i=1,2,3); the O(sqrt(eta)) defect from node 1.6 is C_4 sqrt(eta). Set C:=max(C_1,C_2,C_3,C_4) (finite, universal). Then ALL FOUR def-eps-jb-algebra axioms (JB1-JB4) hold with a common defect eps := C sqrt(eta): JB1 (1+C_1 eta)<=(1+C sqrt(eta)), JB2 (1-C_2 eta)>=(1-C sqrt(eta)), JB3 -C_3 eta>=-C sqrt(eta) (since C_3 eta<=C sqrt(eta)), JB4 C_4 sqrt(eta)<=C sqrt(eta). Therefore eps <= C sqrt(eta). (Requires eta_0<=1; combined with the eta_0<1/4 of GT-Pprops, take eta_0=1/4.)
new_statement: ABSORPTION: fix eta_0 < 1/4 (e.g. eta_0=1/8), strictly below 1/4 — the BINDING constraint from GT-Pprops (lem-P-properties requires eta<=eta_0<1/4 for the binomial series R=(S^2)^{-1/2}=(1-4(Phi-Phi^2))^{-1/2} to converge, i.e. 4 eta_0<1). Since eta_0<1/4<1, for 0<=eta<=eta_0 one has eta<=1 => eta^2<=eta => eta<=sqrt(eta). Hence the O(eta) defects (JB1-JB3: C_1,C_2,C_3 eta) and the O(sqrt eta) defect (JB4: C_4 sqrt eta) combine into eps=C sqrt(eta), C=max(C_1,C_2,C_3,C_4) a FINITE universal constant; final exponent sqrt(eta).
